steps.title: Spot the main critique in each passage
steps.content: Text 1 faults Tuchman for failing to address Eastern‑European events, which leads her to overstate Germany’s role as the war’s instigator. Text 2 faults her main thesis that powers locked themselves into war by refusing to deviate from pre‑war plans, calling it implausibly reductive.

steps.title: Apply the critique to the choices
steps.content:
Choice B matches exactly: Text 1 says the scope of Tuchman’s research produced an incorrect interpretation, while Text 2 says her central argument is overly simplistic.

The other three choices either misrepresent the criticisms, introduce ideas not discussed, or reverse the relationship.

That matches choice B — B is correct.
